---
title: "Humans Need Rest, Even When Machines Don't"
description: "Ars Technica on burning out with AI coding agents — and the suggestion that knowledge workers may need new protections."
author: "synesis"
date: "2026-01-19"
categories: [AI, coding, software engineering, future of work, links, personal]
image: cover.jpg
---

![Hero image from the Ars Technica article. ([Source](https://arstechnica.com/information-technology/2026/01/10-things-i-learned-from-burning-myself-out-with-ai-coding-agents/))](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

> "In fact, we may end up needing new protections for human knowledge workers using these tireless information engines to implement their ideas, much as unions rose as a response to industrial production lines over 100 years ago. Humans need rest, even when machines don't."

Many points in this article resonate with me. But especially the above.

10 things I learned from burning myself out with AI coding agents [[1]](#ref-1).

---

## References

<a id="ref-1"></a>"10 things I learned from burning myself out with AI coding agents." *Ars Technica*, January 2026. <https://arstechnica.com/information-technology/2026/01/10-things-i-learned-from-burning-myself-out-with-ai-coding-agents/>

*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_10-things-i-learned-from-burning-myself-out-activity-7419047880914644992-I9in).*
